# EspoTek Labrador

> Open-source USB electronics lab bench: a $29 board plus a cross-platform
> app (Windows/macOS/Linux/Raspberry Pi/Android) providing a 2-channel
> oscilloscope (±20 V, 375 kSa/s/ch), 2-channel arbitrary waveform generator
> (0.15–9 Vpp, 1 Hz–1 MHz), 4.5–11 V power supply, 2-channel logic analyzer
> with UART/I2C decoding, and a multimeter. This repository contains all
> hardware designs, firmware, and software.

Start here for AI assistance: the LLM-specific guide is self-contained,
uses exact UI strings and live app limits, and lists the common
misconception traps (Vbase semantics, AC vs DC pins, the two-buffer rule).

## Docs

- [LLM guide](Documentation/llm-guide.md): dense, machine-oriented reference — read this first
- [Getting started](Documentation/getting-started.md): install, first connection, safety
- [Tutorial](Documentation/tutorial.md): first measurements via SG1→OSC1 loopback, then a voltage divider
- [User manual](Documentation/user-manual.md): every page and control of the unified app
- [Pinout reference](Documentation/pinout.md): every pin, electrical limits
- [Troubleshooting](Documentation/troubleshooting.md): detection issues, firmware recovery

## Optional

- [Repository README](README.md): building from source, licences
- [Wiki](https://github.com/espotek-org/Labrador/wiki): original documentation
- [Product page](https://espotek.com/labrador): specifications, purchase
